raw_consumer_statement: Citi mistakenly added a {$9200.00} balance on my credit card and has not removed it. On XX/XX/XXXX, I made a payment to a merchant called Ingenes in the amount of {$4600.00}. On XX/XX/XXXX, I made a second payment to the merchant Ingenes in the amount of {$9200.00}. This was foXXXX XXXX XXXX. I ended up cancelling the service and filed a dispute with Citi. Over the course of three months, Citi investigated my dispute and put conditional credits on my card and then reversed them. I withdrew my dispute for both charges on XX/XX/XXXX, because the merchant refunded me. On XX/XX/XXXX, Citi reversed a second {$9200.00} amount on my card by mistake and now it shows that I owe Citi that {$9200.00}. I confirmed with the merchant that this extra charge did not come from them. I then called Citi and escalated this to a supervisor on XX/XX/XXXX, and spoke to " XXXX '' Reference # XXXX, where I recorded the conversation. Citi admitted to the mistake and said they would remove the charge by XX/XX/XXXX. To this date, Citi has not removed the charge and now my balance is {$9500.00} because of the interest. Citi has not communicated with me or sent a letter to me that they said I would receive. My next bill is due on XX/XX/XXXX, and this needs to be removed so I don't have to make a payment on a balance I do not owe.
